structured_payload_candidate.title: T&R Logic Application for VAV Static Pressure Control
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: VAV air handler with multiple downstream zones
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Example sequence: Td=5 min, T=2 min, I=2, SPtrim=-10 Pa, SPres=15 Pa, SPres-max=37 Pa. Setpoint trims down when requests ≤ 2, responds up when requests > 2, capped at 37 Pa per step.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fan status ON, after 5 min delay.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Every 2 min: if R≤2, decrease setpoint by 10 Pa; if R>2, increase setpoint by (R-2)*15 Pa but max 37 Pa.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Starting 5 minutes after the fan status indicates the supply fan is ON, the sequence will slowly reduce the AHUs static pressure setpoint by 10 Pa (0.04 in. of water) every 2 minutes if R≤I. ... the system will respond by increasing static pressure setpoint by 15 Pa (0.06 in. of water) for every request but no more than a maximum of 37 Pa (0.15 in. of water), regardless of the number of requests.

evidence_quote: See Section 5.1.14.4  for an example of T&R implementation.
anchor_repair_reason: evidence_quote replaced with verbatim section heading line
evidence_text: See Section 5.1.14.4  for an example of T&R implementation.
judge_reason: Directly captures a control sequence example with configurable parameters from Section 5.1.14.4 of Guideline 36.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Trim & Respond Set-Point Reset Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Air handler or plant controlling pressure, temperature, or other variables
structured_payload_candidate.summary: T&R logic resets a setpoint at a fixed rate until a downstream zone is no longer satisfied and generates a request. When sufficient requests are present, the setpoint is increased. When requests subside, the setpoint resumes decreasing.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Associated device is proven ON, starting Td after initial device start command.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Every time step T, if R ≤ I, trim setpoint by SPtrim. If R > I, respond by changing setpoint by (R – I)*SPres but no larger than SPres-max.
configurable_values: SPres (respond amount, opposite sign to SPtrim)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Trim & Respond logic shall reset the setpoint within the range SPmin to SPmax. When the associated device is OFF, the setpoint shall be SP0. The reset logic shall be active while the associated device is proven ON, starting Td after initial device start command. When active, every time step T, if R≤I, trim the setpoint by SPtrim. If there are more than I requests, respond by changing the setpoint by SPres*(R – I), but no more than SPres-max.

evidence_quote: 5.1.14.  Trim & Respond Set -Point Reset Logic
anchor_repair_reason: evidence_quote replaced with verbatim section heading line
evidence_text: 5.1.14.  Trim & Respond Set -Point Reset Logic
judge_reason: The item describes a real control sequence (Trim & Respond logic) from section 5.1.14, which is operational knowledge per Guideline 36.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Request-Hours Accumulator Reset
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: T&R set-point reset logic and zone/system reset requests
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Request-Hours Accumulator and System Run-Hours Total are reset to zero automatically when System Run-Hours Total exceeds 400 hours, or manually by a global operator command.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: System Run-Hours Total exceeds 400 hours, or manual command.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset Request-Hours Accumulator and System Run-Hours Total to zero.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Request-Hours Accumulator and System Run-Hours Total are reset to zero as follows: i. Reset automatically for an individual zone/system when the System Run-Hours Total exceeds 400 hours. ii. Reset manually by a global operator command.

evidence_quote: The Request-Hours Accumulator and System Run-Hours Total are reset to zero as follows: i. Reset automatically for an individual zone/system when the System Run-Hours Total exceeds 400 hours. ii. Reset manually by a global operator command.
evidence_text: The Request-Hours Accumulator and System Run-Hours Total are reset to zero as follows: i. Reset automatically for an individual zone/system when the System Run-Hours Total exceeds 400 hours. ii. Reset manually by a global operator command.
judge_reason: Captures a specific, operational commissioning procedure regarding automatic and manual reset of request-hour accumulators based on system run-hours, which is a configurable control parameter from the guideline.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Rogue Zone Detection via Cumulative% Request-Hours
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: T&R set-point reset logic and zone/system reset requests
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Downstream zones or systems generating requests
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A Level 4 alarm is generated if zone Importance-Multiplier > 0, Cumulative% Request-Hours > 70%, and total zone/system run hours > 40.

structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A Level 4 alarm is generated if the zone Importance-Multiplier is greater than zero, the zone/system Cumulative% Request Hours exceeds 70%, and the total number of zone/system run hours exceeds 40.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Primary Only Condensing Boiler Staging Down

Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity, QbX, of the boilers in the next available lower stage for 5 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If all boilers enabled in the current stage are condensing, stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. For 5 minutes, Primary HW pumps are at B-MinPriPumpSpdStage and primary HWRT exceeds secondary HWRT by 3°F. 2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true. 3. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity, QbX, of the boilers in the next available lower stage for 5 minutes.

judge_reason: Extraction captures a real control sequence with configurable parameters and timing conditions from Section 5.21.3.9.m, providing actionable operational knowledge.
